content.text:
[
  {
    "ruleId": "T2",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "where": "page, section 'Double the arrow, double the number?', sentence 2",
    "evidence": "text says 'You already saw length raise the score when c1 stretched' but the only stretch demo on the page named the candidate 'Night Drive' (via 'grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size'); the label 'c1' is never introduced anywhere on the page, so the referent is unresolvable",
    "suggestion": "use the same name the widget used, e.g. 'you already saw length raise the score when Night Drive stretched'"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "T2",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "where": "page, section 'Double the arrow, double the number?', sentences 4 and 6",
    "evidence": "the page instructs 'point the probe straight along c1 and set its length back to 1.0 first' and 'Double c1's length exactly, same direction' - 'c1' is not a label present in the interactive widget (which shows Night Drive, Glass Rain, Slow Burn, Static), so the reader has no way to identify which control corresponds to 'c1' to carry out the instruction",
    "suggestion": "replace 'c1' with the actual widget candidate name used earlier (e.g. Night Drive) so the instruction is executable"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "T2",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "page, section 'What did that sweep actually teach you?', prompt 'Before you scroll on'",
    "evidence": "prompt says 'Say what each reading claims about two vectors' but every prior section on the page used the word 'arrow' exclusively; 'vector' is introduced here without being equated to 'arrow'",
    "suggestion": "say 'two arrows' to keep the page's established vocabulary, or add a one-clause aside equating vector and arrow"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "T2",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "page, extensions menu, 'Argue it out' second prompt",
    "evidence": "text says 'the score's sign alone told you aligned, perpendicular, or opposed' but the page's main lesson body only ever used 'across'/'ignore' for the zero case, never the word 'perpendicular'",
    "suggestion": "use the page's own term ('across' / 'ignore') or briefly gloss perpendicular as the across/ignore case"
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "T2",
    "pass": false,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "where": "beat-03 on-screen text",
    "evidence": "on-screen annotation 'a . b' introduces symbolic labels a and b for the two arrows without any prior narration verbally assigning those letters to probe/candidate",
    "suggestion": "have the narration say 'call the probe's numbers a and the candidate's numbers b' just before the annotation appears"
  }
]
